meta.title: Bluewave 10x Implementation Prompt
meta.purpose: Complete engineering specification to transform Bluewave from a $12/user DAM tool into a $29-149/user AI-native Creative Operations Platform

current_architecture.summary: Multi-tenant SaaS MVP with row-level isolation, JWT auth, FastAPI backend, React+TS frontend, PostgreSQL database. AI features are deterministic stubs behind an AIServiceProtocol interface.

backend.auth: JWT HS256 via python-jose + bcrypt via passlib

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4 with CSS variable design tokens (light/dark)
frontend.http: Axios with JWT interceptor + exponential backoff retry
frontend.ui_components: 13 custom components in src/components/ui/ built on Radix UI

database.multi_tenancy: Row-level via TenantMixin (tenant_id FK) + SQLAlchemy session event auto-filter

infrastructure.orchestration: Docker Compose (3 services: postgres, backend, frontend)

implementation_phases.name: Real AI Integration (Claude API)
implementation_phases.revenue_impact: Enables $0.05/action pricing on top of seat fees

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.description: Claude API key for real AI features

AI_MODEL.description: Default model — use Haiku for speed/cost on captions, Sonnet for complex tasks

AI_ENABLED.description: Feature flag to disable real AI and fall back to stubs

AI_MAX_TOKENS_CAPTION.description: Max output tokens for caption generation

AI_MAX_TOKENS_HASHTAGS.description: Max output tokens for hashtag generation

AI_PRICE_PER_ACTION.description: Price charged to tenant per AI action

backend/app/services/claude_ai_service.p.description: Real AI service implementing AIServiceProtocol using Claude API

generate_caption.signature: async def generate_caption(self, filename: str, file_type: str, tenant_context: dict | None = None) -> str
generate_caption.prompt_strategy: System prompt includes brand voice context if available. User prompt describes the media file. Returns a marketing-ready caption.
generate_caption.fallback: If API fails after retry, fall back to StubAIService result

generate_hashtags.signature: async def generate_hashtags(self, filename: str, file_type: str, tenant_context: dict | None = None) -> list[str]
generate_hashtags.prompt_strategy: Analyze file metadata + any caption context. Return 6-10 relevant hashtags. If tenant has brand hashtags defined, include those.

analyze_image.signature: async def analyze_image(self, image_bytes: bytes, file_type: str) -> dict
analyze_image.description: NEW — Send actual image to Claude vision for rich description, object detection, scene analysis, color palette extraction
analyze_image.returns: { description: str, objects: list[str], scene: str, colors: list[str], mood: str }
analyze_image.note: This is the foundation for smart auto-tagging and brand compliance

check_brand_compliance.signature: async def check_brand_compliance(self, image_bytes: bytes, brand_guidelines: dict) -> ComplianceResult
check_brand_compliance.description: NEW — Compare image against tenant's brand guidelines
check_brand_compliance.returns: ComplianceResult { score: int (0-100), issues: list[ComplianceIssue], passed: bool }
check_brand_compliance.guidelines_input: { colors: list[hex], fonts: list[str], logos: list[url], tone: str, dos: list[str], donts: list[str] }

backend/app/services/claude_ai_service.p.error_handling: Log failures, fall back to stub, never block the upload flow

backend/app/services/ai_factory.py.description: Factory that returns the appropriate AI service based on config
backend/app/services/ai_factory.py.logic: If AI_ENABLED and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set → return ClaudeAIService() else return StubAIService()
backend/app/services/ai_factory.py.singleton: Instantiate once at module level, import everywhere as `ai_service`

changes: Update _run_ai_stubs to pass image bytes to analyze_image() for vision-capable analysis
After AI generates caption/tags, also store the raw analysis in a new JSONB column
Log AI action for usage tracking (see Phase 10 ai_usage_logs table)
Add POST /ai/analyze/{asset_id} — trigger full image analysis (vision)
Add POST /ai/compliance/{asset_id} — run brand compliance check
Add GET /ai/usage — return tenant's AI action count and cost for current billing period
All endpoints log to ai_usage_logs table

ai_usage_logs.description: Track every AI action for billing and analytics

columns.action_type: ENUM(caption, hashtags, analyze, compliance, brand_voice) NOT NULL
columns.cost_usd: DECIMAL(10,6) NOT NULL — Anthropic API cost
columns.price_charged: DECIMAL(10,4) NOT NULL — price charged to tenant

new_columns.ai_analysis: JSONB NULLABLE — raw AI vision analysis result
new_columns.compliance_issues: JSONB NULLABLE — list of compliance issues found
new_columns.auto_tags: ARRAY(String) NULLABLE — AI-detected objects, scenes, etc.

new_columns.ai_actions_used: INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — current billing period counter

hooks: useAIUsage() — GET /ai/usage, returns { actions_used, actions_limit, cost_this_period }

AssetDetailPage.tsx: Show compliance_score as a circular progress indicator (green ≥80, yellow ≥50, red <50)
Show auto_tags as pill badges below hashtags
Add 'Re-analyze with AI' button that triggers full vision analysis
Show ai_analysis.objects and ai_analysis.mood as metadata

new_components.src/components/ui/ComplianceScore.tsx: Circular gauge showing 0-100 score with color coding
new_components.src/components/AIUsageBanner.tsx: Shows 'X of Y AI actions used this month' in sidebar or settings

implementation_phases.name: Brand Compliance Engine
implementation_phases.revenue_impact: Primary differentiator that creates switching cost

columns.logo_urls: ARRAY(String) — paths to uploaded logo files
columns.tone_description: TEXT — 'Professional but approachable, never use slang'
columns.dos: ARRAY(String) — 'Always include the tagline', 'Use sentence case'
columns.donts: ARRAY(String) — 'Never use the old logo', 'Avoid red backgrounds'

backend/app/models/brand_guideline.py.constraint: One active guideline per tenant (soft constraint in app logic)

endpoints.description: Get current tenant's active brand guidelines
Create or update brand guidelines. Accepts JSON body with colors, fonts, tone, dos/donts. Also accepts logo file uploads (multipart).
Run brand compliance check on a specific asset. Calls ClaudeAIService.check_brand_compliance() with the asset image and tenant's guidelines. Returns ComplianceResult. Logs to ai_usage_logs.
Aggregate compliance stats: average score, most common issues, trend over time

models: BrandGuidelineCreate { primary_colors, secondary_colors, fonts, tone_description, dos, donts }
ComplianceResult { score: int, passed: bool, issues: list[ComplianceIssue] }
ComplianceIssue { severity: 'error'|'warning'|'info', category: str, message: str, suggestion: str }

workflow_integration.change: When asset is submitted (draft → pending_approval), automatically run brand compliance check as a background task if guidelines exist. Store score on the asset. If score < configurable threshold (default 50), auto-reject with compliance issues as the rejection comment.

sections: Color palette editor — color picker inputs for primary/secondary, shows swatches
Font list — text inputs for font family names
Logo upload — drag-and-drop zone for logo files, shows thumbnails
Tone description — textarea for voice/tone guidelines
Do's and Don'ts — two-column list with add/remove capability
src/pages/BrandPage.tsx.route: /brand (admin only, add to AppLayout nav and RoleGuard)

AssetDetailPage.tsx: Compliance score badge next to status badge
If score < 80, show expandable list of compliance issues with severity icons
AssetListPage.tsx: Add compliance score as a small indicator on each asset card
Add filter tab: 'Non-compliant' showing assets with score < threshold

implementation_phases.name: White-Label Client Portals
implementation_phases.priority: HIGH — new revenue stream ($29/portal), turns agencies into resellers
implementation_phases.revenue_impact: $29/month per portal add-on. Agency with 10 clients = $290/month new revenue.

columns.name: VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL — e.g. 'Acme Corp Portal'
columns.slug: VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL — URL slug e.g. 'acme-corp'
columns.primary_color: VARCHAR(7) DEFAULT '#2563EB' — hex color for branding

backend/app/models/portal_collection.py.description: Curated collections of approved assets shared with a client

columns.name: VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL — e.g. 'Q1 Campaign Assets'
columns.expires_at: TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE — optional expiry for time-limited shares

backend/app/models/portal_collection_ass.description: Many-to-many: which approved assets belong to which collection

backend/app/models/portal_access_log.py.description: Track client downloads and views for analytics

endpoints: GET /p/{slug} — Portal landing page data (name, logo, color, welcome message)

routing_changes.App.tsx: Add <Route path='/p/:slug/*' element={<PortalPublicPage />} /> BEFORE AuthGuard routes

endpoints.description: Dashboard summary: total assets, assets by status, avg approval time, AI actions used, estimated savings

response.assets_by_status: { draft: int, pending: int, approved: int }
response.avg_approval_hours: float — average time from submit to approve
response.estimated_hours_saved: float — based on benchmark (8.8 hrs/week searching × reduction factor)
response.approval_bottleneck: list[{ user_id, name, avg_review_hours }] — slowest reviewers

endpoints.description: Assets that are approved but never downloaded or shared — waste detection
Assets with similar AI analysis results — deduplication candidates

computation_notes.estimated_hours_saved: Formula: (total_assets_this_period × avg_search_time_without_dam) - (total_assets_this_period × avg_search_time_with_dam). Industry benchmark: 8.8 hrs/week → reduced to ~2 hrs/week with DAM. Per asset: ~15 min saved on search + 10 min on metadata = 25 min saved per asset. At 200 assets/month = 83 hours saved.
computation_notes.estimated_money_saved: hours_saved × $50/hr (configurable per tenant — creative team hourly rate)

src/pages/AnalyticsPage.tsx.description: ROI Dashboard with charts and key metrics

sections.name: ROI Banner
sections.description: Top section: large number showing 'Estimated savings this month: $X,XXX' with hours saved beside it. Green if trending up.

sections.name: Key Metrics Grid
sections.description: 4 stat cards: Total Assets, Avg Approval Time, AI Actions Used, Compliance Score. Each shows current value + trend arrow (up/down from last period).

sections.name: Activity Charts

sections.name: Team Performance
sections.description: Table showing each user: uploads, approvals, avg review time, rejection rate. Sortable columns.

sections.name: Waste Detection
sections.description: Card showing unused approved assets count with 'Review' link. Duplicate asset pairs with merge suggestion.

implementation_phases.name: Brand Voice AI Training
implementation_phases.revenue_impact: $0.25 per Brand Voice generation (premium AI pricing)

columns.name: VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Default Voice'
columns.training_data_count: INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — number of approved captions used
columns.style_summary: TEXT NULLABLE — AI-generated summary of the brand's writing style
columns.example_captions: JSONB NULLABLE — top 20 highest-quality approved captions as few-shot examples
columns.system_prompt_override: TEXT NULLABLE — custom system prompt for this brand voice

backend/app/services/brand_voice_trainer.description: Analyzes approved captions to build a brand voice profile

train.signature: async def train(self, tenant_id: UUID) -> BrandVoiceProfile
logic: 1. Fetch all approved assets with captions for the tenant
2. Send the top 50 captions to Claude Sonnet with a meta-prompt: 'Analyze these captions and describe the brand voice: tone, vocabulary, sentence structure, common patterns, preferred phrases'
3. Claude returns a structured analysis — store as style_summary + tone_keywords + vocabulary
4. Select the 20 best captions (longest, most detailed) as few-shot examples
5. Generate a system_prompt_override that encodes the brand voice for future caption generation
train.minimum_data: Requires at least 30 approved captions. Show progress indicator: '12/30 captions needed for Brand Voice training.'

generate_with_voice.signature: async def generate_with_voice(self, filename: str, file_type: str, profile: BrandVoiceProfile) -> str
generate_with_voice.logic: Use the profile's system_prompt_override + few-shot examples as context when calling Claude for caption generation. This produces captions that match the tenant's established voice.

integration.ai_service: ClaudeAIService.generate_caption() checks if tenant has an active BrandVoiceProfile. If yes, uses generate_with_voice() instead of generic generation. Charges $0.25 instead of $0.05.
integration.automatic_retraining: After every 50 new approved captions, schedule a background retrain task. The voice improves over time.

BrandPage.tsx: New section: 'Brand Voice AI' showing training status
Progress bar: 'X/30 approved captions — Y more needed to train'
If trained: show style_summary, tone_keywords, example captions
Toggle: 'Use Brand Voice for all AI captions' (on/off)
Preview: 'Generate test caption with Brand Voice' — shows before/after comparison (generic vs. brand voice)

implementation_phases.data_moat_explanation: After 6 months of use, the BrandVoiceProfile contains a deep understanding of the tenant's writing style. Switching to a competitor means losing this trained model and starting from scratch. This is the single most powerful retention mechanism.

implementation_phases.name: Content Calendar & Scheduling
implementation_phases.revenue_impact: Included in Business tier — reduces need for separate scheduling tool

backend_changes.description: Mostly uses existing publish_events table with scheduled_at column. Add a scheduler background task.

backend/app/services/scheduler.py.description: Background task that checks for publish_events where scheduled_at <= now() AND status == 'scheduled', then publishes them
backend/app/services/scheduler.py.method: Run every 60 seconds via a startup background task or Celery beat
backend/app/services/scheduler.py.logic: Query pending scheduled events → for each, call the appropriate publisher → update status to published or failed

GET /api/v1/calendar.response: List of publish_events with asset thumbnails, channel info, status — grouped by date

frontend_changes.uses: CalendarPage.tsx from Phase 14 — extend with drag-and-drop scheduling and weekly/monthly views

new_pricing_implementation.description: Implement tier-based feature gating and usage-based billing

columns.tenant_id: UUID FK tenants.id UNIQUE NOT NULL
columns.plan: ENUM(free, pro, business, enterprise) NOT NULL DEFAULT free
columns.status: ENUM(active, cancelled, past_due) NOT NULL DEFAULT active
columns.ai_actions_limit: INTEGER NULLABLE — NULL = unlimited (enterprise), free = 50, pro = unlimited, business = unlimited
columns.portal_limit: INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — free=0, pro=1, business=5, enterprise=unlimited

feature_gating_middleware.description: FastAPI dependency that checks the tenant's subscription plan before allowing premium features

dependencies.require_plan: def require_plan(*allowed_plans: str) — raises 403 if tenant not on required plan
dependencies.check_ai_quota: async def check_ai_quota(tenant_id) — raises 429 if AI action limit exceeded
dependencies.check_portal_quota: async def check_portal_quota(tenant_id) — raises 403 if portal limit reached
dependencies.check_storage_quota: async def check_storage_quota(tenant_id, file_size) — raises 413 if storage limit exceeded

stripe_integration.description: For payment processing — Phase 18 or outsource to Stripe Billing
endpoints: POST /api/v1/billing/portal — Create Stripe Customer Portal session (manage subscription)

object_storage.what: Replace local volume with S3-compatible storage (MinIO for self-hosted or AWS S3)
object_storage.why: Local filesystem won't scale for multi-channel distribution, CDN delivery, and client portals
object_storage.implementation: Create StorageProtocol with LocalStorage and S3Storage implementations. Swap via config flag.

task_queue.what: Replace FastAPI BackgroundTasks with Celery + Redis (or ARQ for async)
task_queue.why: AI calls, image processing, publishing, and automation execution need distributed task processing with retries, rate limiting, and monitoring

caching.what: Add Redis for API response caching and rate limiting
caching.why: Analytics queries, portal public pages, and AI quota checks need fast lookups

celery_beat.purpose: Scheduled tasks: AI retraining, engagement data fetching, publish scheduling

new_tables: ai_usage_logs — Track every AI action for billing
brand_guidelines — Tenant brand rules (colors, fonts, tone, dos/donts)
brand_voice_profiles — AI-trained brand voice per tenant
client_portals — White-label portals for agency clients
portal_collections — Curated asset collections in portals
portal_collection_assets — M2M linking assets to collections
publish_events — Scheduled and completed publish actions
subscriptions — Billing plan and feature gating
database_migration_summary.migration_command: docker compose exec backend alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'phase_N_description'

execution_order.description: Strict build order — each phase depends on the previous
execution_order.critical_path: Phase 10 → 11 → 12 → 13 is the fastest path to revenue. 10 weeks to 4 new revenue streams.
